title: heyfood audio transcription contract v1
description: The single canonical client-side contract for the authenticated /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint. The CLI generates its request limits, capture negotiation, and response validation from this document so it can never build a WAV the server would reject and never trust a malformed success body. The audio is transcribed and discarded server-side; it is never retained by the CLI or written to disk.

x-heyfood-audio.notes: max_audio_bytes bounds the WAV file itself; max_request_bytes bounds the whole multipart envelope (WAV + boundary framing + form fields) so multipart overhead can never reject an otherwise-valid maximum-size WAV. The client caps recordings to keep the WAV within max_audio_bytes and refuses to send an envelope larger than max_request_bytes. max_audio_bytes must be >= max_duration_seconds * sample_rate_max_hz * channels * sample_width_bytes + wav_header_bytes, and max_request_bytes must exceed max_audio_bytes.

transcript.description: The recognized text of the recording. Must contain at least one non-whitespace character.

duration_seconds.description: Length of the submitted audio, derived from the WAV header.

language.description: BCP-47 language tag of the transcript (e.g. "en"), or null when the server does not report one.

model_version.description: Opaque transcription version tag (e.g. "hf-transcribe-1"). Not a provider or model identifier; treat it as a stable, meaningless-to-parse label.
